About

National data, made clear and usable for everyone.

I'm a federal data scientist and data steward. At the U.S. Department of Education, I look after national Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA) Section 618 data: the figures every state reports each year on the students it serves in special education.

I run the Discipline and Personnel collections, co-produce the Office of Special Education Programs (OSEP) Fast Facts, serve on two federal data-governance boards, and build accessible, Section 508-compliant dashboards, increasingly powered by AI. I'm also a published researcher, with work in journals like Autism and Exceptional Children, and I founded and lead the Autism and Neurodivergency Advocacy Association. (Views my own, not my employer's.)

I collect this data, check that it is correct, help share it in easy-to-read public reports, and build tools that make it usable for everyone. I also do research and lead a nonprofit that supports neurodivergent people. (These views are my own.)

Career journey

2024 to now
current role

Data Analyst (Education Program Specialist)

U.S. Department of Education, Office of Special Education Programs

I serve as one of the key data stewards and subject-matter experts for Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA) Section 618 reporting: the federal data that every U.S. state submits each year on the infants, children, and students it serves in special education. My work spans six national data collections that shape how the country understands and funds services for students with disabilities. (Views my own, not my employer's.)

  • Run the IDEA Discipline (2023–24, 2024–25) and Personnel (2024–25) collections and their static tables. In plain terms: the Discipline data tracks how often students with disabilities are suspended, expelled, or removed from class, and the Personnel data counts the special-education teachers and staff who serve them. Together they show how students with disabilities are served nationwide.
  • The collections I work on affect 8M+ students with disabilities, counting infants and toddlers served under IDEA Part C alongside the school-age students under Part B: I assist regularly on the Assessment collection (state-test performance) and on Maintenance of Effort and Coordinated Early Intervening Services (MOE/CEIS), and have previously assisted with the Exiting, Child Count, and Educational Environments collections.
  • The data I steward underpins the IDEA program that sends about $15 billion in federal formula funds to states each year, so getting it right shapes how much support reaches classrooms.
  • Co-authored the four most recent OSEP Fast Facts releases, turning IDEA Section 618 data into accessible, Section 508-compliant data stories for the public.
More highlights
  • Contributed data to and served on the team responsible for the IDEA Annual Report to Congress, the federal government's yearly report on the state of special education across the United States.
  • Built Python automation for data-quality checks and validation, plus reusable Section 508-compliant templates now used across the Office of Special Education Programs.
  • Represent the Office of Special Education Programs on two federal data-governance boards: the EDFacts Data Governance Board and the Office of Planning, Evaluation and Policy Development (OPEPD) Data Literacy Workgroup.
  • Oversee 15 grants worth more than $20M a year, including two national special-education data centers.
  • Co-delivered a keynote to 1,200 people at the OSEP Leadership Conference, with additional talks at the DaSy Center and Center for IDEA Fiscal Reporting (CIFR).
  • Member of the Federal Interagency Working Group on Autism (FIWA), which coordinates autism research and services across United States agencies.
2021 to now

Founder & CEO

Autism & Neurodivergency Advocacy Association

Founder and Chief Executive Officer of a 501(c)(3) nonprofit advancing neurodiversity through research, education, and international partnerships. Produced a Neurodiversity Awareness Day with Columbia University that drew more than 300 attendees.

2021 to 2024

Intern

U.S. Department of Education, Institute of Education Sciences

A three-year internship at the Institute of Education Sciences (IES), the Department's statistics and research arm, including research with restricted-use NAEP data. Spent the final six months at the Office of Special Education Programs, right before joining OSEP full time.

2021 to 2024

Graduate Research Fellow

Teachers College, Columbia University

Conducted quantitative and qualitative research on autism and special education, co-authoring peer-reviewed studies published in Autism and Exceptional Children.

2023 to 2025

Inclusive Education Co-Chair

UNESCO · Sustainable Development Goal 4 Youth & Student Network

Selected from more than 1,500 applicants to represent youth in global education policy. Authored policy briefs and led trainings on disability inclusion at international forums.

2023 to 2024

International Disability Rights Intern

U.S. Department of State

Supported the Office of International Disability Rights on a narrative-change initiative advancing the human rights of people with disabilities, coordinating across public affairs and diplomatic channels.

2021 to 2022

Intern

Office of the Director of National Intelligence

Helped design and stand up the intelligence community's Autism at Work initiative, a hiring and onboarding program that brings autistic professionals into intelligence-community roles.

2022

Data Science / Analytics Intern

NASA · Goddard Space Flight Center

Applied ARIMA (time-series) models to social-media analytics and built dashboards that the Hubble Space Telescope team continues to use.

2021

Data Science Intern

Geisinger

Built regression models across 90,000 patient records to study autism and co-occurring genetic disorders, and produced COVID-19 forecasting to support operational planning.

2019 to 2020

Computational Astrophysics Researcher

NASA Kepler · Bloomsburg University

Led an independent study of NASA Kepler Space Telescope time-series data using Fourier analysis, presenting findings at four academic conferences.

Skills

Languages & query
Python, R, SQL, SPSS, Java Full professional: Python, R · Professional working: SQL, SPSS · Limited working: Java
AI & GenAI
Generative AI (certified), Machine Learning with Python (certified), AI-assisted analytics, prompt engineering
Machine learning & statistics
Machine learning, predictive modeling, random forest, neural networks, time series / ARIMA, regression, data automation
Platforms & BI
Power BI, Looker / Google Data Studio, Excel
UX & accessibility
Section 508 accessibility, accessible dashboards, data visualization, data storytelling
Research methods
Quantitative & qualitative research, latent class analysis, content analysis, data analysis

Selected work

IDEA Section 618 Data Collections

U.S. Department of Education, Office of Special Education Programs

Lead the 2023–24 and 2024–25 Discipline and Personnel collections and their static tables, and support the MOE/CEIS, Assessment, and Part C Child Count collections. QC, validation, and Section 508-accessible public displays.

See the collections on ED’s Open Data Platform

OSEP Fast Facts

U.S. Department of Education, Office of Special Education Programs

Authored the four most recent OSEP Fast Facts releases, turning IDEA Section 618 data into accessible, Section 508-compliant data stories for the public.

Read the OSEP Fast Facts

IDEA Annual Report to Congress

U.S. Department of Education, Office of Special Education Programs

Contributed Section 618 data to, and served on the team responsible for, the IDEA Annual Report to Congress, the federal government's yearly report on the state of special education in the United States.

Read the 47th Annual Report to Congress (PDF)

Python QC & Automation Toolkit

U.S. Department of Education, Office of Special Education Programs

Built Python automation for scoring-report parsing and quality-control checks, plus reusable Section 508-compliant reporting templates adopted across the division.

IDEA Data Dashboard & Data Storytelling

Built on public IDEA Section 618 data

Built an interactive dashboard and a scrollytelling site that turn public IDEA Section 618 data into easy national and state views, with plain-language guides, drill-down popups, a build-your-own-chart tool, and a built-in chatbot that answers questions about the data in plain language.

Visa Outcome Prediction Engine

U.S. Department of State (intern)

Developed the data-scraping and modeling logic for a neural network predicting visa application outcomes; led the intern team through validation.

Language Use in Autism Research

Published in Autism (SAGE)

Systematic analysis of 12,962 abstracts across 11 autism journals, tracing the shift from person-first to identity-first language.

Read the paper in Autism

Special Education Goals of Autistic Students

Published in Exceptional Children

Latent class analysis of IEP goals with demographic and developmental covariates, mapping how educational needs cluster.

Read the paper in Exceptional Children

Out-of-School Reading Habits of Autistic Students

Poster at the Institute of Education Sciences (IES) · with Matthew Zajic

Latent profile analysis of restricted-use NAEP data on the out-of-school reading habits of 810 autistic 8th graders, from my IES internship.

Meta-synthesis of autistic adults' mental-health service experiences

Published in Autism in Adulthood, 2026

A meta-synthesis of how autistic adults experience mental-health services, with Andoni, Eisenhower, and Levitt.

Read the paper in Autism in Adulthood

The strengths of speech divergence in autistic people

Published in Infant and Child Development, 2025

On the strengths of speech divergence in autistic people, written with Kapp.

Read the paper in Infant and Child Development

Population Health Regressions

Geisinger

Ran regressions across 90,000 patient records on autism-linked genetic disorders, plus COVID-19 supply and trend forecasting.

Full list on Google Scholar: 50 citations · h-index 3

Curious Universe Podcast Analytics

NASA · Goddard

Built an analytics dashboard for NASA's Curious Universe podcast, tracking episode performance and audience growth, and contributed insights to the show's team.

Episodes I supported: The Search for Life: Are We Alone? · Mysteries of the Moon · How to Grow Plants in Space · Up & Away with Sounding Rockets

Hubble Social Analytics

NASA

Built a dashboard and statistical report using R and Google Data Studio, including ARIMA forecasting. Insights helped shape social media strategy and the dashboard is still used by the team.

Kepler Light-Curve Analysis

NASA · Kepler

Interpolated gaps and ran Fourier transforms on stellar time-series data from the Kepler Space Telescope.

Neurodiversity Awareness Day

Autism & Neurodivergency Advocacy Assn.

Produced a 300+ attendee event with Columbia advancing neurodiversity inclusion, education, and community.

Public speaking

I speak on accessible data, data storytelling, and neurodiversity, from United Nations panels to university stages. I'm open to public speaking as a side role, outside my federal work.

The Right to Higher Education A panel on higher education for students with disabilities at United Nations Headquarters. Image: Juliette Gudknecht on a United Nations panel about the right to higher education for students with disabilities.
Invited talk at the University of Oxford A talk on participatory autism research, using stigma and anti-ableist language as a case study (2024). Image: Juliette Gudknecht presenting at the University of Oxford.
OSEP Leadership Conference keynote Co-delivered to 1,200 people at the U.S. Department of Education, Office of Special Education Programs. Image: Juliette Gudknecht co-delivering the OSEP Leadership Conference keynote.
Safe spaces for autistic students A talk for the Global Campaign for Education, a worldwide coalition of education-advocacy groups. Image: Juliette Gudknecht speaking at the Global Campaign for Education.
International Day of Education Posed autism-advocacy questions to international leaders at United Nations Headquarters. Image: Juliette Gudknecht at the International Day of Education at United Nations Headquarters.
Introduction to Neurodiversity The annual Neurodiversity Awareness Day at Columbia University (2024). Image: Juliette Gudknecht presenting at the 2024 Neurodiversity Awareness Day at Columbia University.
Neurodiversity Awareness Day opening Co-hosted the 2023 event at Columbia University with Ara Bakhteyar. Image: Juliette Gudknecht co-hosting the 2023 Neurodiversity Awareness Day at Columbia University.

Invite me to speak: keynotes, panels, and workshops on neurodiversity and accessible data.

Contact

Want to work together?

I take on the occasional side project or speaking engagement when it stays clear of my federal ethics rules, and I am open to the right new role. If you are working on something in data, machine learning, AI, or accessible technology, or would like me to speak, send me a note.

Accessibility
Your choices are saved on this device.
The IDEA dashboard A walk through the leadership briefing, chart builder, state profiles, insights, and chatbot.

Privacy policy

Last updated June 2026

This is a personal portfolio site. It does not collect personal information, run analytics or advertising trackers, or set tracking cookies.

On-device settings. If you change the accessibility options (reduce motion, larger text, and so on), those choices are stored only in your own browser using local storage so the site remembers them on your next visit. They never leave your device and are never sent anywhere. You can clear them anytime with the “Reset” button in the accessibility panel.

Contact & links. If you email me, I receive whatever you choose to send (your name, email address, and message) and use it only to reply. This site also links to outside services like LinkedIn, Google Scholar, and YouTube, and the talk videos are YouTube embeds that load from YouTube only when you press play. Once you follow a link or play a video, that service runs under its own privacy policy, not mine.

Questions? Email juliettegudknecht14@gmail.com. Views expressed here are my own and not those of my employer.
